data.content:
## Incremental Build Analysis

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11) — **Always invalidates**
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** → MSBuild always re-executes it.
- Uses `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` — a **volatile value** that changes every build, so `build-timestamp.txt` is always rewritten with new content, invalidating downstream targets like `CoreCompile`.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23) — **Always re-runs, often invalidates**
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** → MSBuild always re-executes it.
- `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` **updates the file timestamp** on every build even when the git hash hasn't changed, which can trigger recompilation.
- Neither generated file (`build-timestamp.txt`, `git-hash.g.cs`) is added to `FileWrites`, so `dotnet clean` won't remove them.

### Fixes

For **`GenerateTimestamp`**: either remove it (timestamps inherently break incrementality) or add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and accept it will still always run due to the volatile property.

For **`EmbedGitHash`**: add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and use a **write-if-changed** pattern — write to a `.tmp` file first, then `Copy` only if content differs:

```xml
<Target Name="EmbedGitHash"
        BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFile)"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs">
  <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
    <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
  </Exec>
  <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs.tmp" Lines="..." Overwrite="true" />
  <!-- Only overwrite if content actually changed -->
  <Copy SourceFiles="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs.tmp"
        DestinationFiles="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
        SkipUnchangedFiles="true" />
  <ItemGroup>
    <FileWrites Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
    <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Target>
```

**Root cause summary**: both targets lack `Inputs`/`Outputs` and use volatile or unconditionally-overwritten files, guaranteeing a full rebuild every time.
data.reasoningText:
I can see two custom targets that have incremental build issues. Let me analyze them:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp`** (line 6-11): This target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` and writes the current timestamp to a file. Since `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` produces a different value every time, the file content changes on every build, which causes downstream targets to re-run. Additionally, this target has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild will always execute it.

2. **`EmbedGitHash`** (line 13-23): This target also runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"`. It runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes a generated `.cs` file. While the git hash doesn't change as often as a timestamp, this target also lacks `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so it always re-runs. More critically, it writes to `git-hash.g.cs` on every build, and even if the git hash hasn't changed, `Write

` updates the file timestamp, which can trigger recompilation. The generated file also isn't registered in `FileWrites`, so `Clean` won't remove it.

The core problems are that both targets lack `Inputs`/`Outputs` declarations to enable incremental build skipping, and both write files that change timestamps even when content is identical, forcing unnecessary recompilation.
